ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF PERSONS MOST QUALIFIED AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-10-2010 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

DISCOVERY 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF PETER ZAPP JR.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS UNUM GROUP AND THE PAUL REVERE LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, SET TWO IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT REVIVE FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS BY REASKING THEM AFTER THE TIME FOR MOTION PRACTICE HAD PASSED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE : PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 301, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES. PLAINTIFF PETER ZAPP'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF PERSONS MOST QUALIFIED FOR DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS AS A GROUP MUST PRODUCE IN SAN FRANCISCO AT LEAST ONE PERSON MOST QUALIFIED FOR EACH OF PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION CATEGORIES BY OCTOBER 22, 2010. PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MEET AND CONFER CONCERNING THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED AT DEPOSITION. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1750, AS REASONABLE REIMBURSEMENT BECAUSE DEFENDANTS' REFUSAL TO PRODUCE WAS WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. SAID SANCTION SHALL BE PAID WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE : PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED.

Mtn To Compel Production Of Persons Most Qualified For Depo; Req For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALICE J. WOLFSON OF DL GROUP, TEL. NO. 271-7169 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. FRANCIS J. TORRENCE OF WILSON ELSER & DICKER LLP, TEL. NO. 433-0990 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES. PLAINTIFF PETER ZAPP's Motion To Compel Production Of Persons Most Qualified For Deposition and Request For Sanctions is granted. Defendants as a group must produce in San Francisco at least one person most qualified for each of Plaintiff's deposition categories by October 22, 2010. Parties are directed to meet and confer concerning the documents to be produced at deposition. Plaintiff is awarded sanctions in the amount of $1750, as reasonable reimbursement because Defendants refusal to produce was without substantial justification. Said sanction shall be paid within 20 days of this Order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge : Peter J. Busch, NOT REPORTED. =(301/PJB)
